AI.motivation: 现有轻量级语义分割方法在多尺度解码器中独立计算每个特征尺度的注意力，导致大量冗余计算，因为跨尺度的注意力分布高度相关。
AI.method: 提出CSAP框架，仅在最深特征尺度计算注意力，然后将得到的注意力图传播到较浅阶段，完全绕过这些阶段的查询-键值计算，保留多尺度上下文推理同时大幅降低计算成本。
AI.result: CSAP-Tiny在ADE20K上达到42.9% mIoU（仅5.5 GFLOPs），Cityscapes上80.5%（21.5 GFLOPs），COCO-Stuff 164K上40.9%（5.5 GFLOPs），比SegNeXt-Tiny在ADE20K上提升+1.8%同时减少16.8%浮点运算。
AI.conclusion: CSAP框架通过跨阶段注意力传播有效减少语义分割解码器的计算冗余，在保持多尺度上下文推理能力的同时显著提升计算效率，为轻量级语义分割提供了有效的解决方案。

title: Synergizing Efficiency and Reliability for Continuous Mobile Manipulation
summary: Humans seamlessly fuse anticipatory planning with immediate feedback to perform successive mobile manipulation tasks without stopping, achieving both high efficiency and reliability. Replicating this fluid and reliable behavior in robots remains fundamentally challenging, not only due to conflicts between long-horizon planning and real-time reactivity, but also because excessively pursuing efficiency undermines reliability in uncertain environments: it impairs stable perception and the potential for compensation, while also increasing the risk of unintended contact. In this work, we present a unified framework that synergizes efficiency and reliability for continuous mobile manipulation. It features a reliability-aware trajectory planner that embeds essential elements for reliable execution into spatiotemporal optimization, generating efficient and reliability-promising global trajectories. It is coupled with a phase-dependent switching controller that seamlessly transitions between global trajectory tracking for efficiency and task-error compensation for reliability. We also investigate a hierarchical initialization that facilitates online replanning despite the complexity of long-horizon planning problems. Real-world evaluations demonstrate that our approach enables efficient and reliable completion of successive tasks under uncertainty (e.g., dynamic disturbances, perception and control errors). Moreover, the framework generalizes to tasks with diverse end-effector constraints. Compared with state-of-the-art baselines, our method consistently achieves the highest efficiency while improving the task success rate by 26.67\%--81.67\%. Comprehensive ablation studies further validate the contribution of each component. The source code will be released.

AI.tldr: 提出一个统一框架，将效率与可靠性协同结合，实现连续移动操作任务
AI.motivation: 人类能够无缝融合预期规划和即时反馈来执行连续移动操作任务，而机器人复制这种流畅可靠行为面临挑战，因为追求效率会损害不确定性环境中的可靠性
AI.method: 提出可靠性感知轨迹规划器，将可靠执行要素嵌入时空优化；结合相位依赖切换控制器，在全局轨迹跟踪和任务误差补偿间无缝切换；采用分层初始化促进在线重规划
AI.result: 真实世界评估表明，该方法能在不确定性下高效可靠完成连续任务，相比基线方法在保持最高效率的同时，任务成功率提升26.67%至81.67%
AI.conclusion: 该框架成功协同了效率与可靠性，实现了连续移动操作，并能泛化到具有不同末端执行器约束的任务中

title: Bridging Natural Language and Microgrid Dynamics: A Context-Aware Simulator and Dataset
summary: Addressing the critical need for intelligent, context-aware energy management in renewable systems, we introduce the \textbf{OpenCEM Simulator and Dataset}: the first open-source digital twin explicitly designed to integrate rich, unstructured contextual information with quantitative renewable energy dynamics. Traditional energy management relies heavily on numerical time series, thereby neglecting the significant predictive power embedded in human-generated context (e.g., event schedules, system logs, user intentions). OpenCEM bridges this gap by offering a unique platform comprising both a meticulously aligned, language-rich dataset from a real-world PV-and-battery microgrid installation and a modular simulator capable of natively processing this multi-modal context. The OpenCEM Simulator provides a high-fidelity environment for developing and validating novel control algorithms and prediction models, particularly those leveraging Large Language Models. We detail its component-based architecture, hybrid data-driven and physics-based modelling capabilities, and demonstrate its utility through practical examples, including context-aware load forecasting and the implementation of online optimal battery charging control strategies. By making this platform publicly available, OpenCEM aims to accelerate research into the next generation of intelligent, sustainable, and truly context-aware energy systems.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了OpenCEM仿真器和数据集，这是首个专门设计用于将丰富的非结构化上下文信息与定量可再生能源动态相结合的开源数字孪生平台。
AI.motivation: 传统能源管理严重依赖数值时间序列，忽视了人类生成上下文（如事件日程、系统日志、用户意图）中嵌入的重要预测能力，需要智能、上下文感知的可再生能源管理系统。
AI.method: 开发了OpenCEM平台，包括来自真实世界光伏-电池微电网安装的精心对齐的语言丰富数据集，以及能够原生处理这种多模态上下文的模块化仿真器，采用基于组件的架构和混合数据驱动与物理建模能力。
AI.result: 创建了OpenCEM仿真器和数据集，提供了高保真环境用于开发和验证新颖的控制算法和预测模型，特别是那些利用大型语言模型的算法，并通过上下文感知负载预测和在线最优电池充电控制策略等实际示例展示了其实用性。
AI.conclusion: 通过公开提供OpenCEM平台，旨在加速下一代智能、可持续且真正上下文感知的能源系统研究，为可再生能源管理中的自然语言与动态系统集成提供了重要工具。

title: Pre-Execution Safety Gate & Task Safety Contracts for LLM-Controlled Robot Systems
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used to convert task commands into robot-executable code, however this pipeline lacks validation gates to detect unsafe and defective commands before they are translated into robot code. Furthermore, even commands that appear safe at the outset can produce unsafe state transitions during execution in the absence of continuous constraint monitoring. In this research, we introduce SafeGate, a neurosymbolic safety architecture that prevents unsafe natural language task commands from reaching robot execution. Drawing from ISO 13482 safety standard, SafeGate extracts structured safety-relevant properties from natural language commands and applies a deterministic decision gate to authorize or reject execution. In addition, we introduce Task Safety Contracts, which decomposes commands that pass through the gate into invariants, guards, and abort conditions to prevent unsafe state transitions during execution. We further incorporate Z3 SMT solving to enforce constraint checking derived from the Task Safety Contracts. We evaluate SafeGate against existing LLM-based robot safety frameworks and baseline LLMs across 230 benchmark tasks, 30 AI2-THOR simulation scenarios, and real-world robot experiments. Results show that SafeGate significantly reduces the acceptance of defective commands while maintaining a high acceptance of benign tasks, demonstrating the importance of pre-execution safety gates for LLM-controlled robot systems

AI.tldr: 本文提出SafeGate神经符号安全架构，用于在LLM控制的机器人系统中检测和阻止不安全的自然语言任务命令，防止其转化为可执行代码。
AI.motivation: 当前LLM将任务命令转换为机器人可执行代码的流程缺乏验证机制，无法在执行前检测不安全或有缺陷的命令，且即使初始安全的命令在执行过程中也可能产生不安全的状态转换。
AI.method: 提出SafeGate神经符号安全架构，基于ISO 13482安全标准从自然语言命令中提取结构化安全相关属性，应用确定性决策门授权或拒绝执行；引入任务安全合约将命令分解为不变量、守卫和中止条件；结合Z3 SMT求解器进行约束检查。
AI.result: 在230个基准任务、30个AI2-THOR仿真场景和真实机器人实验中评估，SafeGate显著减少了缺陷命令的接受率，同时保持了对良性任务的高接受率。
AI.conclusion: SafeGate证明了预执行安全门对于LLM控制的机器人系统的重要性，能够有效防止不安全命令到达机器人执行阶段，提高系统安全性。

title: ALTO: Adaptive LoRA Tuning and Orchestration for Heterogeneous LoRA Training Workloads
summary: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) is now the dominant method for parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models, but achieving a high-quality adapter often requires systematic hyperparameter tuning because LoRA performance is highly sensitive to configuration choices. In practice, this leads to many concurrent LoRA jobs, often spanning heterogeneous tasks in multi-tenant environments. Existing systems largely handle these jobs independently, which both wastes computation on weak candidates and leaves GPUs underutilized. We present ALTO (Adaptive LoRA Tuning and Orchestration), a co-designed training system that accelerates LoRA hyperparameter tuning while enabling efficient cluster sharing across heterogeneous tasks. The central insight behind ALTO is that when multiple tuning jobs run concurrently over a shared frozen backbone, they expose optimization opportunities that single-job designs cannot exploit. Building on this, ALTO monitors loss trajectories to terminate unpromising configurations early, uses fused grouped GEMM together with a new rank-local adapter parallelism to co-locate surviving adapters and reclaim freed GPU capacity, and combines intra-task and inter-task scheduling to improve multi-task placement by leveraging the predictable duration of LoRA jobs. Extensive evaluation shows that ALTO achieves up to $13.8\times$ speedup over state-of-the-art without sacrificing adapter quality.

AI.tldr: 本文提出ALTO系统，用于加速LoRA超参数调优并实现异构任务间的高效集群共享
AI.motivation: LoRA性能对配置选择高度敏感，需要大量超参数调优实验，导致并发LoRA任务众多且异构，现有系统独立处理这些任务造成计算资源浪费和GPU利用率低下
AI.method: ALTO通过监控损失轨迹提前终止无望配置，使用融合分组GEMM和新的秩局部适配器并行化来共置存活适配器，结合任务内和任务间调度改进多任务放置
AI.result: ALTO相比最先进方法实现了高达13.8倍的加速，且不牺牲适配器质量
AI.conclusion: ALTO通过利用多个调优任务在共享冻结骨干网络上运行时的优化机会，显著加速LoRA超参数调优并提高集群资源利用率

title: PRISM-MCTS: Learning from Reasoning Trajectories with Metacognitive Reflection
summary: PRISM-MCTS: Learning from Reasoning Trajectories with Metacognitive Reflection Siyuan Cheng, Bozhong Tian, Yanchao Hao, Zheng Wei Published: 06 Apr 2026, Last Modified: 06 Apr 2026 ACL 2026 Findings Conference, Area Chairs, Reviewers, Publication Chairs, Authors Revisions BibTeX CC BY 4.0 Keywords: Efficient/Low-Resource Methods for NLP, Generation, Question Answering Abstract: The emergence of reasoning models, exemplified by OpenAI o1, signifies a transition from intuitive to deliberative cognition, effectively reorienting the scaling laws from pre-training paradigms toward test-time computation. While Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) has shown promise in this domain, existing approaches typically treat each rollout as an isolated trajectory. This lack of information sharing leads to severe inefficiency and substantial computational redundancy, as the search process fails to leverage insights from prior explorations. To address these limitations, we propose PRISM-MCTS, a novel reasoning framework that draws inspiration from human parallel thinking and reflective processes. PRISM-MCTS integrates a Process Reward Model (PRM) with a dynamic shared memory, capturing both "Heuristics" and "Fallacies". By reinforcing successful strategies and pruning error-prone branches, PRISM-MCTS effectively achieves refinement. Furthermore, we develop a data-efficient training strategy for the PRM, achieving high-fidelity evaluation under a few-shot regime. Empirical evaluations across diverse reasoning benchmarks substantiate the efficacy of PRISM-MCTS. Notably, it halves the trajectory requirements on GPQA while surpassing MCTS-RAG and Search-o1, demonstrating that it scales inference by reasoning judiciously rather than exhaustively.

AI.tldr: 提出PRISM-MCTS推理框架，通过元认知反思从推理轨迹中学习，提高推理模型的效率和效果
AI.motivation: 现有基于蒙特卡洛树搜索的推理方法将每次搜索视为独立轨迹，缺乏信息共享，导致效率低下和计算冗余，无法充分利用先前探索的洞察
AI.method: 提出PRISM-MCTS框架，集成过程奖励模型和动态共享内存，捕捉启发式和谬误，通过强化成功策略和剪枝易错分支实现精炼，并开发了数据高效的过程奖励模型训练策略
AI.result: 在多样化推理基准测试中验证了PRISM-MCTS的有效性，在GPQA上减少了一半轨迹需求，超越了MCTS-RAG和Search-o1方法
AI.conclusion: PRISM-MCTS通过元认知反思和共享记忆机制，实现了更高效的推理，表明通过明智而非穷举的推理可以扩展推理能力

title: VideoStir: Understanding Long Videos via Spatio-Temporally Structured and Intent-Aware RAG
summary: Scaling multimodal large language models (MLLMs) to long videos is constrained by limited context windows. While retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a promising remedy by organizing query-relevant visual evidence into a compact context, most existing methods (i) flatten videos into independent segments, breaking their inherent spatio-temporal structure, and (ii) depend on explicit semantic matching, which can miss cues that are implicitly relevant to the query's intent. To overcome these limitations, we propose VideoStir, a structured and intent-aware long-video RAG framework. It firstly structures a video as a spatio-temporal graph at clip level, and then performs multi-hop retrieval to aggregate evidence across distant yet contextually related events. Furthermore, it introduces an MLLM-backed intent-relevance scorer that retrieves frames based on their alignment with the query's reasoning intent. To support this capability, we curate IR-600K, a large-scale dataset tailored for learning frame-query intent alignment. Experiments show that VideoStir is competitive with state-of-the-art baselines without relying on auxiliary information, highlighting the promise of shifting long-video RAG from flattened semantic matching to structured, intent-aware reasoning. Codes and checkpoints are available at Github.

AI.tldr: 提出VideoStir框架，用于解决多模态大语言模型在长视频理解中因上下文窗口限制而面临的挑战，通过结构化检索增强生成技术提升长视频理解能力。
AI.motivation: 现有方法存在两个主要问题：一是将视频扁平化为独立片段，破坏了固有的时空结构；二是依赖显式语义匹配，可能错过与查询意图隐式相关的线索。需要克服这些限制以实现更好的长视频理解。
AI.method: 提出VideoStir框架，首先将视频构建为片段级别的时空图，然后执行多跳检索以聚合跨距离但上下文相关的事件证据。引入基于MLLM的意图相关性评分器，根据帧与查询推理意图的对齐程度进行检索。为此构建了IR-600K大规模数据集用于学习帧-查询意图对齐。
AI.result: 实验表明VideoStir在不依赖辅助信息的情况下与最先进的基线方法具有竞争力，突显了将长视频RAG从扁平化语义匹配转向结构化、意图感知推理的前景。
AI.conclusion: VideoStir通过结构化时空图和意图感知检索，成功解决了长视频理解中的关键挑战，为从扁平化语义匹配转向结构化意图感知推理提供了有前景的方向。

title: Multi-Agent Pathfinding with Non-Unit Integer Edge Costs via Enhanced Conflict-Based Search and Graph Discretization
comment: 16 pages, 7 figures, submitted tocs.AI, Multi-Agent Systems, Pathfinding Optimization
summary: Multi-Agent Pathfinding (MAPF) plays a critical role in various domains. Traditional MAPF methods typically assume unit edge costs and single-timestep actions, which limit their applicability to real-world scenarios. MAPFR extends MAPF to handle non-unit costs with real-valued edge costs and continuous-time actions, but its geometric collision model leads to an unbounded state space that compromises solver efficiency. In this paper, we propose MAPFZ, a novel MAPF variant on graphs with non-unit integer costs that preserves a finite state space while offering improved realism over classical MAPF. To solve MAPFZ efficiently, we develop CBS-NIC, an enhanced Conflict-Based Search framework incorporating time-interval-based conflict detection and an improved Safe Interval Path Planning (SIPP) algorithm. Additionally, we propose Bayesian Optimization for Graph Design (BOGD), a discretization method for non-unit edge costs that balances efficiency and accuracy with a sub-linear regret bound. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach outperforms state-of-the-art methods in runtime and success rate across diverse benchmark scenarios.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种新的多智能体路径规划变体MAPFZ，用于处理具有非单位整数边成本的图，并开发了高效的求解方法。
AI.motivation: 传统MAPF方法假设单位边成本和单时间步动作，限制了实际应用；MAPFR虽然处理非单位成本但导致无限状态空间，影响求解效率。需要一种既能处理非单位成本又保持有限状态空间的方法。
AI.method: 提出MAPFZ变体处理非单位整数边成本；开发CBS-NIC框架，包含基于时间间隔的冲突检测和改进的SIPP算法；提出BOGD图离散化方法，平衡效率与精度。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在运行时间和成功率方面优于现有方法，在各种基准场景中表现优异。
AI.conclusion: MAPFZ在保持有限状态空间的同时提高了现实性，提出的求解框架和离散化方法有效解决了非单位整数边成本的MAPF问题。

title: Learning to Synergize Semantic and Geometric Priors for Limited-Data Wheat Disease Segmentation
summary: Wheat disease segmentation is fundamental to precision agriculture but faces severe challenges from significant intra-class temporal variations across growth stages. Such substantial appearance shifts make collecting a representative dataset for training from scratch both labor-intensive and impractical. To address this, we propose SGPer, a Semantic-Geometric Prior Synergization framework that treats wheat disease segmentation under limited data as a coupled task of disease-specific semantic perception and disease boundary localization. Our core insight is that pretrained DINOv2 provides robust category-aware semantic priors to handle appearance shifts, which can be converted into coarse spatial prompts to guide SAM for the precise localization of disease boundaries. Specifically, SGPer designs disease-sensitive adapters with multiple disease-friendly filters and inserts them into both DINOv2 and SAM to align their pretrained representations with disease-specific characteristics. To operationalize this synergy, SGPer transforms DINOv2-derived features into dense, category-specific point prompts to ensure comprehensive spatial coverage of all disease regions. To subsequently eliminate prompt redundancy and ensure highly accurate mask generation, it dynamically filters these dense candidates by cross-referencing SAM's iterative mask confidence with the category-specific semantic consistency derived from DINOv2. Ultimately, SGPer distills a highly informative set of prompts to activate SAM's geometric priors, achieving precise and robust segmentation that remains strictly invariant to temporal appearance changes. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that SGPer consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance on wheat disease and organ segmentation benchmarks, especially in data-constrained scenarios.

AI.tldr: 该论文提出SGPer框架，用于解决小麦病害分割中数据有限和生长阶段外观变化大的问题，通过协同语义和几何先验实现精确分割。
AI.motivation: 小麦病害分割面临两个主要挑战：1) 不同生长阶段存在显著的类内时间变化，导致外观差异大；2) 收集代表性训练数据集既费力又不切实际。需要一种能在有限数据下处理外观变化的方法。
AI.method: SGPer框架将病害分割视为疾病特定语义感知和边界定位的耦合任务。使用DINOv2提供语义先验，通过疾病敏感适配器对齐DINOv2和SAM的表示，将DINOv2特征转化为密集的类别特定点提示，动态过滤冗余提示，激活SAM的几何先验实现精确分割。
AI.result: 广泛评估表明，SGPer在小麦病害和器官分割基准测试中始终达到最先进的性能，特别是在数据受限的场景下，能够严格保持对时间外观变化的不变性。
AI.conclusion: SGPer通过协同语义和几何先验，有效解决了小麦病害分割中的数据有限和外观变化问题，为精准农业中的病害监测提供了鲁棒且精确的解决方案。

title: Training Without Orthogonalization, Inference With SVD: A Gradient Analysis of Rotation Representations
summary: Recent work has shown that removing orthogonalization during training and applying it only at inference improves rotation estimation in deep learning, with empirical evidence favoring 9D representations with SVD projection. However, the theoretical understanding of why SVD orthogonalization specifically harms training, and why it should be preferred over Gram-Schmidt at inference, remains incomplete. We provide a detailed gradient analysis of SVD orthogonalization specialized to $3 \times 3$ matrices and $SO(3)$ projection. Our central result derives the exact spectrum of the SVD backward pass Jacobian: it has rank $3$ (matching the dimension of $SO(3)$) with nonzero singular values $2/(s_i + s_j)$ and condition number $κ= (s_1 + s_2)/(s_2 + s_3)$, creating quantifiable gradient distortion that is most severe when the predicted matrix is far from $SO(3)$ (e.g., early in training when $s_3 \approx 0$). We further show that even stabilized SVD gradients introduce gradient direction error, whereas removing SVD from the training loop avoids this tradeoff entirely. We also prove that the 6D Gram-Schmidt Jacobian has an asymmetric spectrum: its parameters receive unequal gradient signal, explaining why 9D parameterization is preferable. Together, these results provide the theoretical foundation for training with direct 9D regression and applying SVD projection only at inference.

AI.tldr: 本文通过梯度分析研究旋转表示在深度学习中的训练问题，特别是SVD正交化在训练期间的影响以及为什么在推理时使用SVD优于Gram-Schmidt。
AI.motivation: 现有研究表明在训练期间移除正交化、仅在推理时应用可以改善旋转估计，但缺乏理论解释为什么SVD正交化会损害训练，以及为什么在推理时SVD优于Gram-Schmidt。
AI.method: 对3×3矩阵和SO(3)投影的SVD正交化进行详细的梯度分析，推导SVD反向传播雅可比矩阵的精确谱，并分析6D Gram-Schmidt雅可比矩阵的不对称谱。
AI.result: SVD反向传播雅可比矩阵的秩为3，非零奇异值为2/(s_i + s_j)，条件数为(s_1 + s_2)/(s_2 + s_3)，当预测矩阵远离SO(3)时梯度失真最严重。6D Gram-Schmidt雅可比矩阵具有不对称谱，参数接收不等梯度信号。
AI.conclusion: 研究结果为使用直接9D回归进行训练、仅在推理时应用SVD投影提供了理论基础，解释了为什么9D参数化优于6D，以及为什么在训练期间移除SVD可以避免梯度失真问题。

title: CRISP: Rank-Guided Iterative Squeezing for Robust Medical Image Segmentation under Domain Shift
summary: Distribution shift in medical imaging remains a central bottleneck for the clinical translation of medical AI. Failure to address it can lead to severe performance degradation in unseen environments and exacerbate health inequities. Existing methods for domain adaptation are inherently limited by exhausting predefined possibilities through simulated shifts or pseudo-supervision. Such strategies struggle in the open-ended and unpredictable real world, where distribution shifts are effectively infinite. To address this challenge, we introduce an empirical law called ``Rank Stability of Positive Regions'', which states that the relative rank of predicted probabilities for positive voxels remains stable under distribution shift. Guided by this principle, we propose CRISP, a parameter-free and model-agnostic framework requiring no target-domain information. CRISP is the first framework to make segmentation based on rank rather than probabilities. CRISP simulates model behavior under distribution shift via latent feature perturbation, where voxel probability rankings exhibit two stable patterns: regions that consistently retain high probabilities (destined positives according to the principle) and those that remain low-probability (can be safely classified as negatives). Based on these patterns, we construct high-precision (HP) and high-recall (HR) priors and recursively refine them under perturbation. We then design an iterative training framework, making HP and HR progressively ``squeeze'' to the final segmentation. Extensive evaluations on multi-center cardiac MRI and CT-based lung vessel segmentation demonstrate CRISP's superior robustness, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods with striking HD95 reductions of up to 0.14 (7.0\% improvement), 1.90 (13.1\% improvement), and 8.39 (38.9\% improvement) pixels across multi-center, demographic, and modality shifts, respectively.

AI.tldr: 提出CRISP框架，用于解决医学图像分割在域偏移下的鲁棒性问题，通过基于排序而非概率的分割方法提升模型在未见环境中的性能。
AI.motivation: 医学影像中的分布偏移是临床AI翻译的主要瓶颈，现有方法通过模拟偏移或伪监督来穷举预定义可能性，但在开放不可预测的真实世界中效果有限，分布偏移实际上是无限的。
AI.method: 基于"正区域排序稳定性"经验法则，提出CRISP框架，通过潜在特征扰动模拟分布偏移下的模型行为，构建高精度和高召回先验，并设计迭代训练框架使两者逐步"挤压"到最终分割结果。
AI.result: 在多中心心脏MRI和CT肺血管分割的广泛评估中，CRISP表现出卓越的鲁棒性，显著优于最先进方法，在多中心、人口统计和模态偏移下分别实现HD95减少0.14、1.90和8.39像素的改进。
AI.conclusion: CRISP是首个基于排序而非概率的分割框架，无需目标域信息，参数无关且模型无关，能有效应对医学图像分割中的域偏移问题，提升模型在真实世界中的鲁棒性。
